2604.24076.title: An Information-Geometric Framework for Stability Analysis of Large Language Models under Entropic Stress
2604.24076.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in high-stakes and operational settings, evaluation strategies based solely on aggregate accuracy are often insucient to characterize system reliability. This study proposes a thermodynamic inspired modeling framework for analyzing the stability of LLM outputs under conditions of uncertainty and perturbation. The framework introduces a composite stability score that integrates task utility, entropy as a measure of external uncertainty, and two internal structural proxies: internal integration and aligned reective capacity. Rather than interpreting these quantities as physical variables, the formulation is intended as an interpretable abstraction that captures how internal structure may modulate the impact of disorder on model behavior. Using the IST-20 benchmarking protocol and associated metadata, we analyze 80 modelscenario observations across four contemporary LLMs. The proposed formulation consistently yields higher stability scores than a reduced utilityentropy baseline, with a mean improvement of 0.0299 (95% CI: 0.02470.0351). The observed gain is more pronounced under higher entropy conditions, suggesting that the framework captures a form of nonlinear attenuation of uncertainty. We do not claim a fundamental physical law or a complete theory of machine ethics. Instead, the contribution of this work is a compact and interpretable modeling perspective that connects uncertainty, performance, and internal structure within a unied evaluation lens. The framework is intended to complement existing benchmarking approaches and to support ongoing discussions in AI safety, reliability, and governance.
2604.24076.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）在高風險和操作環境中的應用越來越普遍，僅基於總體準確度的評估策略往往不足以表徵系統的可靠性。這項研究提出了一個受熱力學啟發的建模框架，用於分析LLM輸出在不確定性和擾動條件下的穩定性。該框架引入了一個綜合穩定性評分，整合了任務效用、作為外部不確定性度量的熵，以及兩個內部結構代理：內部整合和對齊反射能力。這些量並不是被解釋為物理變量，而是作為一種可解釋的抽象，捕捉內部結構如何調節無序對模型行為的影響。利用IST-20基準測試協議及相關元數據，我們分析了四個當代LLM中的80個模型場景觀察。所提出的公式始終產生比降低的效用熵基線更高的穩定性評分，平均改善為0.0299（95% CI: 0.02470.0351）。在較高熵條件下觀察到的增益更為明顯，這表明該框架捕捉到了一種不確定性的非線性衰減形式。我們並不聲稱有基本的物理法則或完整的機器倫理理論。相反，這項工作的貢獻是一種簡潔且可解釋的建模視角，將不確定性、性能和內部結構連接在一個統一的評估視野中。該框架旨在補充現有的基準測試方法，並支持在AI安全性、可靠性和治理方面的持續討論。
2604.24076.author: Hikmat Karimov et.al.
